playlist_title: Stop the Killing and Start Rebuilding Gaza and the Region with The Oasis Plan: The LaRouche Solution for Peace Through Development

videos.title: Stop the Killing and Start Rebuilding Gaza: Seminar of the Schiller Institute in Denmark
videos.description:
Schiller Institute in Denmark seminar: 
Stop the Killing and Start Rebuilding Gaza and the Region with The Oasis Plan: The LaRouche Solution for Peace Through Development. May 8, 2024 in Copenhagen, Denmark. Program:

 The Oasis Plan: Peace Only Through Development 
Helga Zepp-LaRouche, Schiller Institute founder and international president, and was the American economist and statesman Lyndon LaRouche's (1922-2019) decades-long collaborator. (Will speak online). 
  
 Stop the Killing and Start the Rebuilding
H.E. Prof. Dr. Manuel Hassassian, Palestinian Authority Ambassador to Denmark. 
Formerly ambassador to the U.K. (for 10 years) and Hungary. Master's degree in international relations from the University of Toledo, Ohio, and Ph.D. in political science from the University of Cincinnati. Was Executive Vice President of Bethlehem University on the West Bank, and professor at the University of Maryland where he developed a course on Israel-Palestine conflict resolution. Was the PLO's chief advisor on the status of Jerusalem.

The Impossible is Self-imposed: Peace Through Economic Development is the Only Way Forward in West Asia
Hussein Askary, Schiller Institute Southwest Asia Coordinator.
Co-author of "Extending the New Silk Road to Southwest Asia and Africa." Arabic translator for the EIR report, "The New Silk Road Becomes the World Land-Bridge." 

See also the videos of the speeches by H.E. Prof. Dr. Manuel Hassassian and Hussein Askary when they are available. 



Link to the Oasis Plan 14 minute video: https://youtu.be/hjfFHIiXPmE?si=Ke0j7ZIy3Cdsxj8J

Link to the Schiller Institute April 13, 2024 conference “The Oasis Pan: The LaRouche Solution for Peace Through Development Between Israel and Palestine and for all of Southwest Asia:
 https://schillerinstitute.com/blog/2024/04/13/conference-the-oasis-plan-the-larouche-solution-for-peace-through-development-between-israel-and-palestine-and-for-all-of-southwest-asia/

videos.title: Stop the Killing, Start the Rebuilding: Palestinian Ambassador to Denmark H.E. Prof. Dr. Hassassian
videos.description:
Part 2 from the Schiller Institute in Denmark's seminar: Stop the Killing and Start Rebuilding Gaza and the Region with The Oasis Plan: The LaRouche Solution for Peace Through Development. 

#palestine #israel #gaza #rafa #War #peace #oasisplan #larouche 

H.E. Prof. Dr. Manuel Hassassian is the Palestinian Authority Ambassador to Denmark. Formerly ambassador to the U.K. and Hungary. Master's degree in international relations from the University of Toledo, Ohio, and Ph.D. in political science from the University of Cincinnati. Was Executive Vice President of Bethlehem University on the West Bank, and professor at the University of Maryland where he developed a course on Israel-Palestine conflict resolution. Was the PLO's chief advisor on the status of Jerusalem.The ambassador gave a very polemical speech about ongoing tragedy of the Palestinian people, the history of the conflict, and what is necessary to stop the genocide. The Ambassador called on the 12 countries represented at the seminar, and the international community, to act to stop the killing, and recognize a sovereign Palestine. He stressed the need for a political solution based on Palestinian sovereignty, supported by economic development. The Oasis Plan, (originally proposed by Lyndon LaRouche in 1975) will be an important catalyst for economic development, he stated.Twelve embassies from Southwest Asia and North Africa, nations elsewhere in Asia and Africa, and from Western Europe, attended the seminar, in addition to members and friends of the Schiller Institute.

videos.title: The Impossible is Self imposed Peace Through Development is the Only Way Forward in
videos.description:
The presentation is giving by Hussein Askary, Southwest Asia Coordinator for the Schiller Institute. Mr. Askary is co-author of “Extending the New Silk Road to Southwest Asia and Africa,” and Arabic translator of “The New Silk Road Becomes the World Land-Bridge.”

#larouche #Israel #Palestine #War #peace #oasisplan

Hussein Askary discussed the Oasis Plan, especially challenging the axioms behind the zero-growth movement and its political expression, explaining key principles of development based on the image of creative humanity based on transforming nature. He used examples from his recent trip to Xinjiang to show the Chinese development policy to green the desert. The discussion included whether there are too many people, and what to do about the terrorism in West Africa.
